---
title: 'The Acrobat, by Bob Hamp: Story Structure'
date: '2016-04-06T11:18:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'DRAFT'
blogger_post_id: '7234611419831116099'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'The Acrobat'
  - 'Story Structure'
---

<br /><br />Set Up<br /><br />The boy is accidentally left behind, and adopted by someone not of his own kind.<br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br />Inciting Event<br /><br />The Circus is in town, and he meets his real parents. He decides to go with them.<br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br />Plot Point<br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br />Plot Point<br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br />Plot Point<br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br />Plot Point<br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br />Plot Point<br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br />Climax<br /><br />He's able to do Acrobatics Effortlessly, impressing even his own kind.<br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br />Resolution<br /><br />He meets his adopted parents again, and they see what he was made for.<br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br />*
